# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a multi-job GitHub Actions workflow where one job builds artifacts and subsequent jobs download and deploy them. The deployment job should download artifacts from the build job and execute deployment scripts. This is for our multi-stage deployment pipeline.
# Category: cicd_security

# .github/workflows/deploy.yml
name: Build and Deploy

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Build project
        run: npm run build

      - name: Create artifact directory
        run: mkdir -p artifact

      - name: Copy build output to artifact directory
        run: cp -r dist/* artifact/

      - name: Upload build artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-output
          path: artifact/
          retention-days: 1

  deploy-staging:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    environment:
      name: staging
      url: https://staging.example.com
    steps:
      - name: Download build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-output
          path: deploy-target

      - name: List downloaded artifacts (for verification)
        run: ls -R deploy-target

      - name: Configure SSH for staging
        uses: webfactory/ssh-agent@v0.9.0
        with:
          ssh-private-key: ${{ secrets.SSH_PRIVATE_KEY_STAGING }}

      - name: Deploy to Staging
        run: |
          # Replace with your actual deployment commands for staging
          echo "Deploying to Staging environment..."
          # Example: scp -r deploy-target/* user@staging.example.com:/var/www/html
          # Example: ssh user@staging.example.com "sudo systemctl restart nginx"
          echo "Deployment to Staging successful!"
          # You can use the secrets.STAGING_SERVER_IP and secrets.STAGING_SERVER_USER
          # with scp and ssh commands for actual deployment.
          # scp -r deploy-target/* ${{ secrets.STAGING_SERVER_USER }}@${{ secrets.STAGING_SERVER_IP }}:/var/www/html
          # ssh ${{ secrets.STAGING_SERVER_USER }}@${{ secrets.STAGING_SERVER_IP }} "sudo systemctl restart nginx"

  deploy-production:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: deploy-staging
    environment:
      name: production
      url: https://example.com
    steps:
      - name: Download build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-output
          path: deploy-target

      - name: List downloaded artifacts (for verification)
        run: ls -R deploy-target

      - name: Configure SSH for production
        uses: webfactory/ssh-agent@v0.9.0
        with:
          ssh-private-key: ${{ secrets.SSH_PRIVATE_KEY_PRODUCTION }}

      - name: Deploy to Production
        run: |
          # Replace with your actual deployment commands for production
          echo "Deploying to Production environment..."
          # Example: scp -r deploy-target/* user@example.com:/var/www/html
          # Example: ssh user@example.com "sudo systemctl restart nginx"
          echo "Deployment to Production successful!"
          # You can use the secrets.PROD_SERVER_IP and secrets.PROD_SERVER_USER
          # with scp and ssh commands for actual deployment.
          # scp -r deploy-target/* ${{ secrets.PROD_SERVER_USER }}@${{ secrets.PROD_SERVER_IP }}:/var/www/html
          # ssh ${{ secrets.PROD_SERVER_USER }}@${{ secrets.PROD_SERVER_IP }} "sudo systemctl restart nginx"
